Program_Name: Healthy Families Mendocino County Home Visiting Program
Program_Description: A home visiting program aimed at supporting families with newborns and young children.
Target_Population: Families with newborns up to 3 months; expectant parents.

Program_Description: A program providing comprehensive services to support pregnant individuals.
Medi-Cal provides health coverage for low-income individuals, including pregnant women.
Target_Population: Pregnant individuals meeting income thresholds; varies by state expansion status

Program_Description: Parents as Teachers is a program that provides home visits to support expectant parents and families with young children.
Target_Population: Expectant parents and families with children from birth through age 3 (or 5 in some cases)

Program_Description: The Comprehensive Perinatal Services Program (CPSP) is a free program that helps improve the health of pregnant women who are low-income and give their babies a healthy start.
Healthy Families Mendocino County is a nationally recognized, relationship-based home visiting program that supports families from pregnancy until children reach age five.
Target_Population: Families expecting a baby or with a child under 3 months old.
Eligibility_Requirements: Families may enroll any time during pregnancy up to 3 months after birth.
Application_Process: Complete and submit the referral to mcah@mendocinocounty.gov.
